addcredentialdialog.customApi.desc: Anthropic-compatible upstream relay. Requests are passed through as-is to this address using the key below (zero conversion, equivalent to using that key directly).
addcredentialdialog.field.baseUrl.label: Upstream base URL
addcredentialdialog.field.endpoint.help: Optional. Determines which Kiro API this credential uses. Leave blank to use the global defaultEndpoint
addcredentialdialog.field.endpoint.label: Endpoint
addcredentialdialog.field.endpoint.place: Leave blank to use the default endpoint (e.g. ide / cli)
addcredentialdialog.field.machineId.help: Optional, 64-character hexadecimal string. Leave blank to use the config field, otherwise derived automatically from the Refresh Token
addcredentialdialog.field.machineId.plac: Leave blank to use the config field, otherwise derived automatically from the Refresh Token
addcredentialdialog.field.priority.help: Lower numbers mean higher priority; default is 0
addcredentialdialog.field.priority.label: Priority
addcredentialdialog.field.proxy.help: Supports credentials embedded in the URL (socks5://user:password@host:port), which are auto-detected and split out; you can also use the separate credential fields below. Leave blank to use the global proxy, or "direct" to explicitly bypass the proxy.
addcredentialdialog.field.proxy.label: Proxy Settings
addcredentialdialog.field.proxyUrl.place: e.g. socks5://user:pass@1.2.3.4:1080 (may include credentials) / leave blank for global / direct to bypass proxy
addcredentialdialog.field.region.help: Both may be left blank to use the global config. Auth Region is used for Token refresh, API Region for API requests
addcredentialdialog.field.region.label: Region Settings
addcredentialdialog.field.requestLimit.p: Automatically disable this credential once the cumulative request count is reached
addcredentialdialog.paste.help: Auto-detects single / array / KAM formats and tolerantly corrects common JSON errors. A single failure does not affect the others.
addcredentialdialog.paste.label: Paste JSON in any format
addcredentialdialog.paste.placeholder:
Paste credential JSON here to auto-detect and import.

Supports: single object / array / {credentials:[...]} / KAM export format

[
  { "refreshToken": "...", "clientId": "...", "clientSecret": "..." },
  { "kiroApiKey": "ksk_xxx" }
]

Even if the JSON has minor errors (trailing commas, single quotes, missing brackets), it will attempt to auto-correct them.
addcredentialdialog.title: Add Credential
addcredentialdialog.validate.apiKeyRequi: Please enter your Kiro API Key
addcredentialdialog.validate.baseUrlRequ: Custom API requires the upstream base URL
addcredentialdialog.validate.externalIdp: External IdP requires Client ID and Token Endpoint
addcredentialdialog.validate.idcFieldsRe: IdC/Builder-ID/IAM authentication requires Client ID and Client Secret
balancedialog.title: Credential #{credentialId} balance
batchimportdialog.error.idcNeedsClientId: idc mode requires both clientId and clientSecret
batchimportdialog.hint.autoVerify: 💡 Credentials are verified automatically on import; failed ones are excluded
batchimportdialog.placeholder.jsonInput:
Paste credentials in JSON format (single object or array supported)

OAuth: [{"refreshToken":"...","clientId":"...","clientSecret":"..."}]
API Key: [{"kiroApiKey":"ksk_xxx"}]

The region field is automatically mapped to authRegion
batchimportdialog.title: Batch Import Credentials (Auto Verify)
batchimportdialog.toast.failureSummary: , {failCount} failed ({rollbackSuccess} removed, {rollbackFailed} not removed, {rollbackSkipped} no removal needed)
batchimportdialog.toast.rollbackIncomple: {count} failed credentials could not be rolled back. Please disable and delete them manually
batchverifydialog.hint: Each request is spaced 2 seconds apart to reduce the risk of account bans. You can close this window; verification continues in the background.
batchverifydialog.title: Batch verification
credentialcard.action.refreshTokenApiKey: API Key credentials do not need token refresh
credentialcard.action.refreshTokenTitle: Force refresh Token (works even on disabled accounts, for troubleshooting/recovery)
credentialcard.balanceBar.noData: No data (background gently refreshes every 30 minutes)
credentialcard.cooldown.label: Cooling down
credentialcard.deleteDialog.description: Will be moved to the recycle bin irrecoverably. This action cannot be undone; the credential must be disabled before deletion.
credentialcard.deleteDialog.title: Confirm deletion of credential #{id}?
credentialcard.gearButton.title: Settings (priority / RPM / Profile ARN / enable / delete)
credentialcard.info.allowedModelsTitle: Cost-safety hard gate: this account only accepts these models
credentialcard.info.totalCreditsTitle: Lifetime cumulative credit consumption of this account since joining the pool (real upstream billing, independent of the usage retention period, only increases and never resets)
credentialcard.overageDialog.description: Once enabled, this credential can keep being used after the base quota is exhausted, and the excess is billed by actual usage. Please confirm you understand the billing impact before continuing.
credentialcard.overageDialog.title: Confirm enabling overage?
credentialcard.overageDialog.warning: Overage = billed by actual usage beyond the base quota, may incur extra charges.
credentialcard.settings.aliasPlaceholder: Leave empty to clear, falls back to email / #id
credentialcard.settings.customApiHint: Upstream address + key (leave empty to keep) + request limit. After changing upstream/key you can check to reset the call count. Takes effect on next request.
credentialcard.settings.deleteDisabledTi: The credential must be disabled before it can be deleted
credentialcard.settings.deleteHint: Moves to recycle bin, not recoverable. Must be disabled first.
credentialcard.settings.deleteWarning: Note: turn off "Enable" above before deleting.
credentialcard.settings.description: Alias / proxy / overage / priority / RPM / Profile ARN / enable / reset failures / delete.
credentialcard.settings.profileArnHint: Switch which region's profile this account's conversations use (does not change the global region). Takes effect on next request.
credentialcard.settings.profileArnIdcHin: IdC instances are usually bound to a single region; probing is used to confirm/re-resolve the account's profileArn (usually returns only one region).
credentialcard.settings.proxyHint: Empty = global, "direct" = no proxy; credentials embedded in the URL are auto-split. Takes effect on next request.
credentialcard.settings.resetCountLabel: Also reset call count (recommended after changing upstream/key)
credentialcard.settings.title: Credential settings · #{id}
credentialcard.toast.baseUrlRequired: Upstream address (base URL) cannot be empty
credentialcard.toast.customApiSaved: Custom API config saved, takes effect on next request
credentialcard.toast.disableBeforeDelete: Please disable the credential before deleting
credentialcard.toast.disableOverageUncon: Disable request submitted, but not yet confirmed upstream. Please refresh later to check.
credentialcard.toast.enableOverageUnconf: Enable request submitted, but not yet confirmed upstream. Please refresh later to check.
credentialcard.toast.priorityInvalid: Priority must be a non-negative integer
credentialcard.toast.proxySaved: Proxy saved, takes effect on next request
credentialcard.toast.rpmInvalid: RPM capacity must be a non-negative integer (0 = inherit global)

dashboard.batchWhitelist.noSelection: Please select credentials to set the whitelist first

dashboard.toolbar.batchEnableTip: All selected credentials are already enabled

idclogindialog.form.intro: Sign in to your enterprise account via AWS IAM Identity Center. Enter your Start URL, then complete authorization in your browser.
idclogindialog.form.priorityHelp: The smaller the number, the higher the priority
idclogindialog.form.proxyPlaceholder: Leave empty to use the global proxy

Paste the JSON exported from Kiro Account Manager

logindialog.eidp.fullUrlEmphasis: full URL in the address bar
logindialog.eidp.pasteAuthorizeUrlLabel: Paste the full URL from the address bar after authorizing
logindialog.eidp.pasteSigninUrlLabel: Paste the full URL from the address bar after signing in
logindialog.eidp.regionProbeHint: The Microsoft account's region is detected automatically after authorization. If your account is only enabled in an uncommon region (e.g. eu-central-1), enter it here to probe that region first and avoid missing it. Leave blank if unsure.
logindialog.eidp.regionProbePlaceholder: Leave blank to probe default candidates
logindialog.eidp.selectRegionDesc: This account has a profile in each of several regions. Choose the region to use (it determines which upstream endpoint conversations go through; be sure to pick a region actually enabled for the account).
logindialog.eidp.step1Desc: Open the link below in your browser and sign in to your Microsoft account. After signing in, the browser will redirect to an unreachable localhost:3128 page (a blank or "can't be reached" page is normal). Copy the full URL from that page's address bar and paste it into the input box below.
logindialog.eidp.step1Title: Step 1 / 3 · Sign in to Microsoft account
logindialog.eidp.step2Desc: Open the link below to complete Microsoft authorization. After authorizing, the browser will likewise redirect to an unreachable localhost page (blank or unreachable is normal). Again, copy the full URL from that page's address bar and paste it below.
logindialog.eidp.zeroLocalRun: Nothing runs on your machine: you install and run nothing locally. Just sign in to your Microsoft account in the browser, then copy and paste back the redirected URL from the address bar. The 3 steps below will guide you through it.
logindialog.field.proxyPlaceholder: Leave blank to use the global proxy
logindialog.idc.intro: Sign in via AWS IAM Identity Center. Enter the Start URL, then authorize in the browser.
logindialog.idc.regionHint: It's fine if you get it wrong; the instance's region is detected automatically. Leave the default if unsure.
logindialog.idc.waitingIntro: Open the authorization page in the browser and enter the verification code to complete sign-in.
logindialog.title: Add Account
logindialog.toast.authTimeoutRetry: Authorization timed out, please start again
logindialog.toast.copyFailedSelectManual: Copy failed, please select and copy the link manually
logindialog.toast.noProfileFound: No available profile detected for this account (Kiro/CodeWhisperer may not be enabled)
logindialog.toast.pasteAuthorizeUrl: Please paste the full URL from the browser address bar after authorizing
logindialog.toast.pasteSigninUrl: Please paste the full URL from the browser address bar after signing in
logindialog.web.intro: Sign in to your Kiro account in the browser; once done, the credential is automatically added to the pool.
logindialog.web.waitingBrowser: Waiting for the browser to finish signing in…
logindialog.web.waitingIntro: Click "Open sign-in page" to sign in to your Kiro account in a new tab; once done it is automatically detected and added to the pool.
loginpage.error.connectFailed: Unable to connect to the service, please try again later
modeltestdialog.chip.unknown: Upstream error during probe, cannot determine (retryable)
modeltestdialog.credential.label: Credential #{id}
modeltestdialog.footer.realRequestNote: Each selected model sends one real request with no prompt and consumes real credits (only working ones are billed). Results stay on this page; you can change the selection and test again.
modeltestdialog.footer.testHistoryNote: Historical test results (not tested this run but tested before)
modeltestdialog.history.lastResultHint: Last test results (click Start test above to re-test and refresh)
modeltestdialog.select.hint: Select models to test (multiplier = billing factor, lower is cheaper)
modeltestdialog.title: Test available models ({count} credentials)
modeltestdialog.toast.noSupportedModel: This account has no available models detected and cannot be set as a whitelist
modeltestdialog.toast.whitelistSaved: Credential #{id} whitelist set to {count} available models (cheap requests locked to this account)
modeltestdialog.whitelist.buttonTitle: Set the detected available models as this account's allow-whitelist (cost safety gate: cheap requests locked to this account, never overflow to expensive accounts)
opsdetaildialogs.bgcache.description: In-memory pool of random login-page backgrounds. Click to enlarge, hover the top-right corner to download. Clearing frees memory (they will be re-fetched on the next login).
opsdetaildialogs.bgcache.title: Login background image cache
opsdetaildialogs.trace.description: Server-side filtered and paginated per-request traces. Click a row for the full text; click IP / device / session to apply it as a filter.
opsdetaildialogs.trace.title: Request details
opsdetaildialogs.trash.confirmDescriptio: This will permanently delete and cannot be undone; the credential will be completely removed from the trash. Continue?
opsdetaildialogs.trash.description: Deleted credentials are held here; you can restore them to the pool or purge them permanently. Purging is irreversible.
opsdetaildialogs.trash.title: Credential trash
opsdetaildialogs.usage.description: Last-24h summary + share pie charts + aggregation by model / account (reads local stats, no upstream calls).
opsdetaildialogs.usage.title: Usage log
opspage.agg.guardRolledBack: The guard script has performed a rollback
opspage.agg.rollbackPoint: Rollback point still available (can roll back)
opspage.agg.stablePending: This version not yet confirmed stable (auto-confirmed after running for a while)
opspage.agg.title: Aggregated ops
opspage.confirm.cleanupByRetention: Cleans up data older than each partition's configured default retention period.
opspage.confirm.cleanupIrreversible: This action is irreversible and permanently deletes the corresponding data.
opspage.confirm.cleanupWhole: This partition is cleared wholesale, removing all its contents.
opspage.confirm.deleteDesc: This moves the credential to the trash (restorable from trash; not recoverable after permanent purge). Delete?
opspage.confirm.disableDesc: Once disabled, this account is removed from the scheduling pool and won't be selected for requests (can be re-enabled anytime). Disable?
opspage.confirm.overageDesc: Once enabled, this account keeps serving beyond its base quota, with the excess billed to the upstream by real usage. This incurs real costs. Enable?
opspage.confirm.restartDesc: Restarting briefly interrupts the gateway for a few seconds, during which all requests fail (including ongoing conversations). It auto-recovers in about 3 seconds. Continue?
opspage.confirm.upgradeDesc: It downloads the new binary from GitHub, verifies the sha256, replaces it, and auto-restarts. The service is briefly down for a few seconds during restart. Continue?
opspage.credops.allowedHint: Empty = no restriction; if set, it's a hard gate
opspage.credops.desc: Per-account settings, all applied instantly with no restart.
opspage.credops.descCustom: Per-account settings, all applied instantly with no restart (custom API account: region / overage / model probing not applicable).
opspage.credops.overageHint: Beyond the base quota, billed by real usage
opspage.credops.proxyHint: Empty = fall back to global, "direct" = force direct connection
opspage.credops.regionHint: Switches the upstream profile, not the global region
opspage.credops.title: Ops actions · {label}
opspage.log.noMatchDesc: Adjust the search or module filter
opspage.log.title: Live logs
opspage.pool.emptyDesc: The pool is empty. Add an account on the Credentials page first.
opspage.pool.streamDisconnected: Live stream disconnected, falling back to 10s polling
opspage.pool.subtitlePolling: · Real circuit state + health score (zero upstream, 10s polling)
opspage.pool.subtitleRealtime: · Real circuit state + health score (zero upstream, live)
opspage.pool.title: Pool health
opspage.probeConfirm.desc: It sends a real generation request to each candidate model to probe availability, consuming real credits on this account (usually a few cents) and taking up to tens of seconds. Continue?
opspage.probeConfirm.title: Probe available models for #{id}?
opspage.recovery.atRestWarning: At-rest encryption is enabled, but the last credential write fell back to plaintext (key file read/write failed). Credentials on disk are currently unencrypted. Check key file permissions / disk writability and retry saving, or review the logs.
opspage.recovery.readFailDesc: Self-healing counter endpoint is not responding
opspage.recovery.title: Self-healing engine
opspage.recovery.uptime: · Uptime {uptime} (cumulative since start, resets on restart)
opspage.row.probeModels: Probe available models (⚠️ consumes real credits)
opspage.storage.title: Storage usage
opspage.toast.restartingWarn: Restarting, auto-recovers in about 3 seconds (this connection dropped, which is normal)
opspage.toast.upgrading: Upgrading, auto-restarts and recovers in a few seconds
opspage.toast.upgradingWarn: Upgrade initiated; it will auto-restart if successful (this connection may drop, which is normal)
overviewpage.dashboard.summary.allLimite: All accounts in the pool are throttled. Add accounts or reduce concurrency.
overviewpage.dashboard.summary.highRisk: Some accounts are about to be throttled. Keep an eye on them.
overviewpage.dashboard.title: Throttle health
overviewpage.empty.poolHint: Add credentials and this pool will light up

overviewpage.kpi.successRate24h.label: 24h success rate
overviewpage.kpi.tokens24h.label: 24h Tokens
overviewpage.kpi.totalCreds.label: Total credentials
overviewpage.pool.title: Account pool status
overviewpage.row.recent429.title: Recent 429 count
overviewpage.section.topUsed.title: Top 5 by usage
overviewpage.title: Overview
overviewpage.trend.empty: No request data in this range
overviewpage.trend.subtitle: Requests and success rate within {label}
overviewpage.trend.title: Request trend
proxytestbutton.label: Test
proxytestbutton.title: Test this proxy (exit IP + latency via backend probe)
regionswitcher.button.customSwitchTitle: Build an ARN from the region you typed and switch now (takes effect only if the probe succeeds)
regionswitcher.button.probeTitle: Probe profiles in each region for this account
regionswitcher.empty.noProfile: No usable profile found. This account may not be enabled in this region (new subscription groups can take up to 24h to propagate), or try another region below.
regionswitcher.toast.probeFirst: Click "Probe regions" first — a known profile is needed to build an ARN for a custom region
regionswitcher.toast.probeOkEmpty: Probe complete: no region profiles found
regionswitcher.toast.probeOkNoneUsable: Probe complete: all {total} regions are unavailable (not enabled / verification failed)
regionswitcher.toast.switchSuccess: Profile ARN switched; takes effect on the next request
settingspage.anti.affinity.hint: Prefer the same credential for the same session
settingspage.anti.affinity.label: Session affinity
settingspage.anti.allCooling.hint: When every credential is cooling: on = immediate 429+Retry-After for client backoff (better multi-account); off = short in-gateway wait until one recovers (smoother for single/small pools).
settingspage.anti.allCooling.label: Pool-wide cooldown fast-fail
settingspage.anti.cooldown.hint: Briefly skip a credential after failures. Open the gear for cooldown duration scale.
settingspage.anti.cooldown.label: Cooldown
settingspage.anti.cooldownEnabled.hint: Briefly skip a credential after failures
settingspage.anti.cooldownEnabled.label: Enable cooldown
settingspage.anti.cooldownScale.hint: Scales recoverable short cooldowns. <100 retries faster (throughput vs risk); >100 more conservative. 100 = original duration.
settingspage.anti.cooldownScale.label: Cooldown duration scale (%)
settingspage.anti.dailyMax.label: Daily cap
settingspage.anti.gear.cooldownDesc: Briefly skip a credential after failures to reduce risk. Cooldown scale makes retries more aggressive or conservative. Hot-reload.
settingspage.anti.gear.rateDesc: Human-like pacing to reduce correlation/risk. Daily cap + min interval + jitter. Hot-reload.
settingspage.anti.jitter.hint: Adds random jitter on the min interval for more human pacing (0–50). 0 = fixed interval.
settingspage.anti.jitter.label: Interval jitter (%)
settingspage.anti.minInterval.label: Min request interval (ms)
settingspage.anti.rateLimit.hint: Human-like pacing: daily cap + interval + jitter. Open the gear for details.
settingspage.anti.rateLimit.label: Rate limiting
settingspage.anti.rateLimitEnabled.hint: Human-like pacing: daily cap + request interval
settingspage.anti.rateLimitEnabled.label: Enable rate limiting
settingspage.appearance.cardSize.hint: Credential page card size; row density is automatic (compact ~5, standard ~4, large ~3).
settingspage.appearance.cardSize.label: Credential card size
settingspage.appearance.poolShowDisabled: When off, the pool status view shows only enabled accounts and hides disabled ones.
settingspage.appearance.poolSort.hint: Orders the overview “pool status” list (live data re-sorts; applies after save).
settingspage.appearance.poolSort.label: Pool status sort
settingspage.basic.defaultEndpoint.label: Default endpoint
settingspage.basic.host.label: Listen address (host)
settingspage.basic.port.label: Port
settingspage.basic.region.label: Region

settingspage.export.desc: Export credential tokens as full JSON (re-importable), refreshToken-only text, or copy to clipboard. Includes sensitive fields such as refreshToken / kiroApiKey — keep them safe.
settingspage.export.toast.noRt: This credential has no refreshToken (may be API Key auth)
settingspage.hint.hotReload: Takes effect immediately on save — no restart needed
settingspage.hint.hotReloadParen: (takes effect immediately on save — no restart)
settingspage.hint.presetOrCustomRestart: Presets available, or enter a custom value (restart required)
settingspage.loadBalance.desc: Priority mode uses credentials in priority order; balanced rotates across available credentials. Takes effect immediately on save.
settingspage.loadBalance.priorityInBalan: When on, balanced mode also tiers by priority (lower first); within a tier, health/load balancing still applies and overflows only when the tier is full. Immediate on save.
settingspage.loginBg.enabled.hint: On: random login background images; off: solid gradient only, no external image fetch
settingspage.loginBg.enabled.label: Show login background image
settingspage.loginBg.r18.hint: On: R18 image source (r18=1); off: all-ages (r18=0). Only matters when background images are enabled (immediate; next prefetch uses new params)
settingspage.loginBg.r18.label: R18 image source
settingspage.network.apiKey.hint: x-api-key for downstream clients. Value is not echoed: blank = keep, fill = update. ⚠️ Restart required.
settingspage.network.apiKey.label: userKey (chat API key)
settingspage.network.callback.hint: Remote mode: browser callbacks hit this URL. Required for server deployments or remote Add Account fails. Blank falls back to local mode (restart required).
settingspage.network.callback.label: Add Account callback URL
settingspage.network.proxy.hint: http(s)://host:port or socks5://host:port; blank clears (restart required)
settingspage.network.proxy.label: Global proxy
settingspage.network.proxyPass.hint: Only for authenticated proxies. Blank = keep (not echoed for security; restart required)
settingspage.network.proxyPass.label: Proxy password
settingspage.network.proxyUser.hint: Only for authenticated proxies. Blank = keep (not echoed for security; restart required)
settingspage.network.proxyUser.label: Proxy username
settingspage.page.emptySearch: No matching settings. Try another keyword.
settingspage.page.footerNote: Except load-balancing mode and the privacy switch (immediate), other fields require a service restart after save. Sensitive fields (API/Admin keys, proxy credentials) are not echoed for security: leave proxy credentials blank to keep them; fill in to update. API/Admin keys may still be maintained in the config file.
settingspage.page.title: Settings
settingspage.privacy.field.hint: When off, device/IP/OS/browser info from downstream clients is neither collected nor stored — better privacy (forwarding and usage stats unaffected).
settingspage.privacy.field.label: Collect downstream client fingerprint (device/IP/OS/browser)
settingspage.privacy.toast.off: Downstream client fingerprinting disabled — no longer collected or stored
settingspage.protocol.ccAuto.hint: When Claude Code is detected, /v1 streaming uses buffered dispatch (accurate input_tokens, equivalent to /cc/v1) so CC need not change endpoints
settingspage.protocol.ccAuto.label: Claude Code auto protocol switch
settingspage.protocol.extractThinking.hi: Parse thinking blocks in non-streaming responses
settingspage.protocol.stripEnv.hint: Strip per-request env/git/model-name noise from system before forward to save tokens, improve cache, reduce correlation
settingspage.protocol.stripEnv.label: Strip environment noise
settingspage.refresh.enable.hint: Refresh soon-to-expire tokens in the background to keep refresh off the hot path and smooth bursts
settingspage.refresh.enable.label: Enable proactive refresh
settingspage.refresh.interval.label: Scan interval (seconds)
settingspage.refresh.lead.hint: Refresh in background when remaining token lifetime is below this
settingspage.refresh.lead.label: Lead time (minutes)
settingspage.security.cors.hint: One origin per line, e.g. https://app.example.com. Blank = allow any (public API; restart required)
settingspage.security.cors.label: CORS allowed origins
settingspage.security.encrypt.hint: When on, credentials.json / trash.json are encrypted at rest with a machine-bound key (XChaCha20-Poly1305). Protects against file copy leaks. Immediate rewrite on save; export/import stays plaintext. ⚠️ Ciphertext won’t open on another machine (re-import from plaintext backup). Default off.
settingspage.security.encrypt.label: Encrypt credentials at rest
settingspage.security.ingress.label: Ingress rate limit (req/min/IP)
settingspage.security.ip.hint: One CIDR or IP per line, e.g. 10.0.0.0/8, 127.0.0.1. Blank = no limit. Invalid entries rejected on save (restart required)
settingspage.security.ip.label: IP allowlist
settingspage.security.ipBlock.hint: Requests from these IPs are rejected. Supports exact IP or CIDR. Empty = no blocklist.
settingspage.security.ipBlock.label: IP blocklist (one per line)
settingspage.security.mcBlock.hint: One machine code per line (e.g. MC-a1b2c3d4e5f6, copy it from the Usage page's "By machine" view). Matched requests are rejected (403, response body: sbsbsb!). Matched by real client IP, works behind a reverse proxy, applies immediately on save. Empty = disabled.
settingspage.security.mcBlock.label: Machine-code blocklist (ban)
settingspage.security.maxBody.hint: 0 = unlimited (recommended; large bodies stream without buffering). Non-zero over-limit returns 413 (restart required)
settingspage.security.maxBody.label: Max request body (bytes)
settingspage.security.xff.hint: Enable only behind a trusted reverse proxy (nginx, etc.); otherwise clients can spoof IP to bypass allowlists (restart required)
settingspage.security.xff.label: Trust X-Forwarded-For
settingspage.service.confirmRestartDesc: Restart briefly takes the gateway offline for a few seconds; all requests fail during that window (including in-flight chats). It usually recovers in about 3 seconds. Continue?
settingspage.service.confirmUpgradeDesc: Downloads the new binary from GitHub, verifies sha256, replaces it, and restarts automatically. The gateway is briefly offline during restart. Continue?
settingspage.service.descOta: Check GitHub and upgrade to the latest version in one click (multi-mirror + sha256 verify; auto-restart after upgrade).
settingspage.service.descRestart: Restart the gateway in one click. Service is briefly offline for a few seconds—confirm no critical in-flight client traffic. Recovers in about 3 seconds.
settingspage.service.rollbackDetectedTit: The guard script performed a rollback
settingspage.service.rollbackPoint: Rollback point still available (can roll back)
settingspage.service.stablePending: ⏳ Not yet confirmed stable (auto-confirmed after running for a while)
settingspage.service.toastRestartingDisc: Restarting — recovers in about 3 seconds (this connection dropped, expected)
settingspage.service.toastUpgrading: Upgrading — will auto-restart in a few seconds
settingspage.service.toastUpgradingDisco: Upgrade started; will auto-restart on success (connection may drop, expected)
settingspage.smart.balanceWeight.hint: When on, among equal priority/health/in-flight, slightly prefer accounts with more remaining balance so the pool drains evenly. Soft bias; does not override in-flight fairness.
settingspage.smart.balanceWeight.label: Balance-weighted routing
settingspage.smart.balanceWeightFloor.hi: Factor floor (integer percent 0–100). 50 → full=1.0, half≈0.75, empty=0.5 (~10–20% tweak). Lower = stronger balance effect; 100 = effectively off.
settingspage.smart.burstSecs.hint: Allows short bursts without queuing. Higher is looser. Default 2.
settingspage.smart.burstSecs.label: Token-bucket burst capacity (seconds)
settingspage.smart.credRpm.hint: Inherited when a credential’s own limit is 0; if this is also 0, built-in fallback 30 is used. Soft-caps dense single-account traffic against upstream sliding windows.
settingspage.smart.credRpm.label: Global per-account RPM soft cap
settingspage.smart.desc: Dynamic routing policies for balanced mode. All take effect immediately on save (hot reload). Defaults are already solid — fine-tune or turn off to fall back to plain load balancing.
settingspage.smart.gear.inboundDesc: Token-bucket shapes ingress bursts into controlled RPM so accounts are less likely to trip upstream 429 storms. Auto mode raises/lowers rate from upstream feedback. Hot-reload.
settingspage.smart.gear.rpmDesc: Per-account RPM soft caps and saturation reserve. Global soft cap is inherited when a credential has no own limit. Hot-reload.
settingspage.smart.hardGate.hint: ⚠️ Advanced: when the whole pool’s RPM is saturated, selection waits in-gateway for the shortest recovery window instead of immediately falling back to the soft gate. Default off (soft-gate picks the least-bad account without blocking).
settingspage.smart.hardGate.hintGear: ⚠️ Advanced: when whole-pool RPM is saturated, wait in-gateway for the shortest recovery instead of soft-gate fallback. Default off.
settingspage.smart.hardGate.label: Pool-saturated backpressure wait
settingspage.smart.headroom.hint: Saturation threshold = cap × factor% (integer). 85 leaves 15% buffer so routing reacts before upstream hard limits; softens 60s window edge bursts. 100 = no discount (hard cap). Open the gear for global per-account RPM soft cap, etc.
settingspage.smart.headroom.hintGear: Saturation threshold = cap × factor%. 85 leaves 15% buffer and triggers earlier. 100 = hard cap.
settingspage.smart.headroom.label: RPM headroom factor
settingspage.smart.health429.hint: When on, a 429 / soft upstream limit lowers that account’s health via EWMA so it gets fewer routes, then recovers gradually. Off: occasional 429s do not change routing.
settingspage.smart.health429.label: 429 / rate-limit demotion
settingspage.smart.inboundAuto.hint: On = AIMD rate control (speed up without 429 / halve on 429) toward the highest stable upstream rate; off = fixed target RPM (manual).
settingspage.smart.inboundAuto.label: RPM auto mode
settingspage.smart.inboundEnabled.hint: On = global token bucket before upstream (bursts queue/smooth); off = unlimited send (easier upstream 429s).
settingspage.smart.inboundEnabled.label: Enable inbound shaping
settingspage.smart.inboundMax.hint: Additive increase will not exceed this. Raise after adding accounts.
settingspage.smart.inboundMax.label: Auto RPM max
settingspage.smart.inboundMin.hint: Multiplicative decrease will not go below this.
settingspage.smart.inboundMin.label: Auto RPM min
settingspage.smart.queueTimeout.hint: On queue timeout: on = pass through to upstream (recommended for single-account / high RPM so the gateway doesn’t stall traffic; worst case degrades to unlimited); off = 429 + Retry-After for client backoff (multi-account / strict upstream protection). Default on.
settingspage.smart.queueTimeout.label: Queue timeout passthrough
settingspage.smart.queueWait.hint: After this wait, “timeout passthrough” decides pass-through vs 429. Default 30.
settingspage.smart.queueWait.label: Max queue wait (seconds)
settingspage.smart.reserve.hint: After headroom discount, reserve N extra slots as fixed burst buffer. 0 = no extra reserve. Stacks with headroom.
settingspage.smart.reserve.label: RPM reserve slots
settingspage.smart.targetRpm.hint: Auto: starting point then dynamic; manual: fixed value.
settingspage.smart.targetRpm.label: Target RPM (start / manual fixed)
settingspage.spoof.kiro.label: Kiro version
settingspage.spoof.node.label: Node version
settingspage.spoof.system.label: System version
settingspage.storage.confirmDescDays: You can set retention days: only data older than that is deleted; leave blank to use the service default retention.
settingspage.storage.confirmDescWhole: This partition is cleaned entirely and all contents will be removed.
settingspage.tool.cardSubtitle: Mitigate / fix Claude Code “Invalid tool parameters”. All hot-reload.
settingspage.tool.cleanLeaked.hint: Strip control tokens leaked at line starts (course/課/count/care glues). Conservative: only line-start glues, not body text. Default on
settingspage.tool.cleanLeaked.label: Clean leaked control tokens
settingspage.tool.descMax.hint: Top-level inbound tool description longer than this is safely truncated at char boundaries (save tokens / avoid upstream hidden caps). Nested schema descriptions use 1/5 of this. Default 10000; 0 = no truncate
settingspage.tool.descMax.label: Tool description character limit
settingspage.tool.exposeError.hint: When tool args are illegal and repair fails, do not send bad JSON — emit a clear SSE error so the client can back off/retry (pairs with the above). Default on
settingspage.tool.exposeError.label: Expose tool errors to the client
settingspage.tool.reclaim.hint: When the model emits tool calls as <invoke> text, reassemble into structured tool_use under four safety gates (line-start, not in code fence, declared tool name, fully closed). Unfixable fragments pass as text. Ported from ZyphrZero production. Default on
settingspage.tool.reclaim.label: Reclaim textified invoke (fixes court/Invalid tool parameters)
settingspage.tool.repairJson.hint: When tool args are illegal JSON, try to repair (escape bad backslashes/raw controls, complete truncation) and re-validate before send. Only intervenes on illegal JSON — zero impact on good streams. Default on. Clients stop seeing Invalid tool parameters
settingspage.tool.repairJson.label: JSON repair layer (fixes Invalid tool parameters)
settingspage.tool.strayGuard.hint: If call/count/card/court monopolize consecutive lines past threshold (32), truncate this turn’s text — stops Opus degeneration spam that burns max_tokens and pollutes history. Default on
settingspage.tool.strayGuard.label: Stray-token repeat circuit breaker
settingspage.tool.streamAlign.hint: When streamed tool-call args form illegal JSON, mark failure (consistent with non-stream; no silent success) so clients can retry. Default on; never disables the account
settingspage.tool.streamAlign.label: Align illegal tool assembly to failure state
settingspage.tool.truncation.hint: If upstream truly truncates tool args and repair cannot complete them, do not send partial args — fail so the client retries the whole turn. Changes flow from “send half” to “fail and retry”; default off
settingspage.tool.truncation.label: Truncation cross-turn recovery
settingspage.trash.confirmAllDesc: Everything in the trash will be removed.
settingspage.trash.confirmSelectedDesc: It cannot be recovered from trash afterward.
settingspage.trash.desc: Deleted credentials are kept here. Restore them to the pool or permanently purge. Permanent delete is
sociallogindialog.done.title: Account added
sociallogindialog.form.intro: Sign in to your Kiro account in the browser. When you finish, the credential is added to the pool automatically — no need to copy tokens by hand.
sociallogindialog.form.priorityHelp: The smaller the number, the higher the priority
sociallogindialog.form.proxyPlaceholder: Leave empty to use the global proxy
sociallogindialog.title: Web Login
sociallogindialog.toast.copyFailedSelect: Copy failed — select the link and copy it manually
sociallogindialog.waiting.intro: Click "Open login page" to sign in to Kiro in a new tab. When login finishes, this dialog detects it and adds the credential to the pool. If the button is blocked by the browser, copy the link below and open it yourself.
usagepage.detail.title: Request details
usagepage.machine.copyCodeTitle: Copy machine code (paste into Settings → Reverse-proxy security → Machine-code blocklist to ban this machine)
usagepage.machine.countHint: machines · by IP, same-session roaming merged
usagepage.machine.machineCodeHint: (one code per IP — copy one to ban that IP)
usagepage.machine.ipTooltip: All IPs seen from this machine (not split when the IP changes)
usagepage.machine.noData: No machine data (no active sessions in the last 10 minutes)
usagepage.machine.sessionPickTitle: Filter recent requests by this session
usagepage.machine.sessionPickedTitle: Recent requests filtered by this session (click again to cancel)
usagepage.popover.closeHint: Click elsewhere or press Esc to close
usagepage.recent.searchPlaceholder: Search model / IP / credential / request ID / error…
usagepage.recent.title: Recent requests
usagepage.statsDisabled.text: Usage statistics are disabled. Set usageEnabled: true in the config and restart the service.
usagepage.table.rowTitle: Left-click to expand details · right-click for a popover with everything
usagepage.title: Usage statistics
usagepage.trend.title: Request trend
